metadata.phase: Track B: ftpd Deep Static RE

tests.name: ftpd_binary_info
tests.result: arch=x86, bits=32, nx=False, canary=False, pic=False, relro=partial

tests.name: ftpd_no_nx
tests.result: NX DISABLED — stack/heap shellcode execution possible

tests.name: ftpd_no_canary
tests.result: CANARY DISABLED — stack buffer overflows directly overwrite return address

tests.name: ftpd_no_pie
tests.result: PIE DISABLED — fixed addresses enable reliable ROP/ret2libc

tests.name: ftpd_import_summary
tests.result: 111 imports, 6 exports, 4 unsafe imports, 0 C++ symbols demangled

tests.name: ftpd_sscanf_import
tests.description: sscanf import — format string + overflow risk
tests.result: sscanf at PLT 0x804a0b0 — if format specifiers lack width limits (e.g., %s instead of %255s), stack buffer overflow is trivial

tests.name: ftpd_strncpy_import
tests.description: strncpy import — off-by-one null termination risk
tests.result: strncpy at PLT 0x804a300 — strncpy does NOT null-terminate if src >= n, enabling read overflow

tests.name: ftpd_xref_summary
tests.result: 0 total unsafe function call sites found

tests.name: ftpd_missing_commands
tests.description: FTP commands NOT found in binary strings
tests.result: Missing: ['PASS'] — may be handled differently or unsupported

samples: 530 Please login with USER and PASS

tests.name: ftpd_dangerous_format_strings
tests.result: 1 format strings use %s without width limit — if used with sscanf/sprintf, buffer overflow is trivial

tests.name: ftpd_auto_files
tests.result: 3 auto-execute file references found — FTP uploads of .AUTO.RSC trigger automatic script execution

tests.name: ftpd_splice_usage
tests.result: ftpd uses splice() for data transfer — efficient but may bypass in-process data inspection/filtering

tests.name: ftpd_rop_survey
tests.result: 926 total gadgets, 10 useful patterns across 2 categories

tests.name: ftpd_rop_rich
tests.result: Rich ROP surface: 926 gadgets. Combined with no NX, attacker has BOTH direct shellcode AND ROP options.

tests.name: ftpd_got_targets
tests.result: Writable GOT entries for: opendir, write, snprintf, readdir, malloc, __cxa_atexit, closedir, open, _ZN6string7freeptrEv, close, read

tests.name: ftpd_got_writability
tests.result: No full RELRO + writable GOT = format string → GOT overwrite → RCE. Overwrite system() GOT entry to redirect execution.

tests.name: ftpd_dotdot_search
tests.result: Found '..' references in binary — checking for sanitization

tests.name: ftpd_no_chroot
tests.result: ftpd does not appear to use chroot() — path validation relies on application-level checks (shortenPath/isSensitiveFile) rather than OS-level isolation. This means any bypass of the path validation logic directly exposes the full filesystem.

tests.name: ftpd_attack_surface_overview
tests.result: Total unsafe call sites: 0 (sscanf: 0, strncpy: 0, snprintf: 0, sprintf: 0)

targets.reason: Sensitive file filter — bypass enables access to system config, user credentials, etc.

findings.title: ftpd: All memory protections disabled (no NX, no canary, no PIE)
findings.description: The ftpd binary (34KB, ELF32 i386) has zero exploit mitigations. Any buffer overflow can: (1) inject and execute shellcode on the stack, (2) overwrite the return address without canary detection, (3) use fixed addresses for ROP gadgets. RELRO=partial.

findings.title: ftpd: Executable stack (GNU_STACK rwx)
findings.description: The ftpd binary has an executable stack segment. Any stack buffer overflow enables direct shellcode injection and execution without ROP.

findings.title: ftpd: 4 unsafe function imports
findings.description: Binary imports 4 known-unsafe C functions: snprintf, sscanf, getsockname, strncpy. With no NX, no canary, no PIE — any overflow via these is directly exploitable.

findings.title: ftpd: Auto-execute file handling (.AUTO.RSC, .AUTO.NPK)
findings.description: The ftpd binary references .AUTO.RSC and .AUTO.NPK files. Uploading a file ending in .AUTO.RSC via FTP may trigger automatic RouterOS script import (/import command visible in strings). This is a persistence vector if FTP write access is obtained.

findings.title: ftpd: shortenPath may not check for '..' (path traversal)
findings.description: The shortenPath function does not appear to contain explicit '..' checks in its disassembly. If this is the path sanitization function for CVE-2019-3943, the fix may be incomplete or the check is elsewhere.
